description: At the gate, a traveller sets out along the village path. Once the traveller is under way, 'wepu' follows the going from the present reference situation along a route, stated or unstated. 'roke' (move) needs no path; 'pholeni' (depart) belongs to the leaving point.
articulatory_notes: Raise the back of the tongue and round the lips for voiced 'w', then move forward and unround into stressed middle-height front 'e'. Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release them into high-back rounded 'u'.
sound_symbolism: The lips begin rounded, open through 'e', meet for 'p', and release into 'u'. The little interruption feels like a foot lifting before the next step.
usage_notes: Ablative 'lue' marks the source, 'wea' (TOWARD) the direction, and 'kau' (ALL) a destination understood as reached. A bare 'wepu' leaves those facts to context. Use 'pholeni' when the departure event itself is the point.

semantic_domains.activity: going from a present situation along a path
